title: Alnylam — ATTR — Synthesis
html:
ATTR Amyloidosis: Cross-Study Synthesis

Source Data

This synthesis draws from ZoomRx International PET deliverables across five countries and two waves, plus the US integrated analysis (PET+ATU+PCA) from Q1 2025 and the April 2026 client check-in meetings.

Country Wave 1 (Baseline) Wave 2 (Trend) Key Competitors Tracked
France Q4 2023 (n=45, full report) Q2 2024 (n=11 neuros, full report) AMVUTTRA, ONPATTRO, VYNDAQEL, TEGSEDI
Italy Q4 2023 (n=53) Q2 2024 (n=30, full report) AMVUTTRA, VYNDAQEL
Germany Q4 2023 (n=50) Q4 2024 (n=13, full report) AMVUTTRA, VYNDAQEL
Spain Q4 2023 (n=60) Q4 2024 (n=26, full report) AMVUTTRA, VYNDAQEL
Japan Q4 2023 (n=60) Q4 2024 (n=25, full report) AMVUTTRA, VYNDAQEL/VYNMAC
US Continuous quarterly tracking Q1 2025 integration (n=140) AMVUTTRA, VYNDAMAX, ATTRUBY, WAINUA

Pattern 1: Geographic Divergence in Competitive Positioning

The most striking finding across the international dataset is that AMVUTTRA's competitive position varies dramatically by country, driven by differences in incumbent strength, specialty mix, reimbursement systems, and historical prescribing norms.

AMVUTTRA Market Position by Country (Q4 2024 wave)

Country AMVUTTRA SOV VYNDAQEL SOV Alnylam Reach Pfizer Reach Alnylam Competitive Position
Spain 56% 44% 91% 86% Leading -- Alnylam has overtaken Pfizer in SOV
Germany 47% 53% 71% 71% On par -- tied reach, slightly trailing SOV
Japan 41% 59% 72% 84% Trailing -- Pfizer maintains dominance
France 55% (Alnylam total) 45% 100% (neuros) 73% (neuros) Leading among neurologists (but neuro-focused, not yet capturing cardiology)
Italy ~40% (est. Q2 2024) ~60% (est.) 51% 62% Trailing -- Pfizer maintained higher reach/frequency in W2

Key insight: Spain is the strongest international market for AMVUTTRA, where Alnylam achieved the highest SOV (56%) and reach (91%) of any country. Germany has reached parity. Japan and Italy remain Pfizer-dominant markets. France is uniquely neuro-centric -- Alnylam leads among neurologists but Pfizer dominates cardiologists.

Why Spain Leads

Spain's Q4 2024 data shows a dramatic shift from Wave 1 (Q4 2023) when VYNDAQEL held 65% SOV and AMVUTTRA had only 35%. Between waves, AMVUTTRA's reach surged from 66% to 91%, frequency increased from 2.6 to 3.8, and SOV flipped from 35% to 56%. This is the most successful competitive turnaround in the international dataset. Contributing factors:

  • Very high academic practice setting (96% academic), which tends to favor novel mechanisms
  • AMVUTTRA reps achieved strong CM efficacy and safety message delivery
  • AMVUTTRA's message effectiveness improved significantly (39% to 61% average)

Why Japan Trails

Japan shows the most pronounced Pfizer dominance: VYNDAQEL/VYNMAC maintains 59% SOV, 84% reach, and higher frequency (2.6 vs. 2.1 for AMVUTTRA). This reflects:

  • Japan's long tafamidis history (approved for hATTR-PN there before many markets)
  • Cardiologist-centric practice patterns that favor oral daily dosing
  • AMVUTTRA reaching only 65% of cardiologists vs. 100% for VYNDAQEL in Q4 2024
  • Lower average hATTR patient loads (median ~2 patients per physician vs. 13+ in Spain)
  • However, AMVUTTRA reps are more impactful -- driving 78% improvement in product perception vs. 57% for VYNDAQEL, suggesting the challenge is reach, not message quality

Pattern 2: Neurologist vs. Cardiologist Divide

Across every country, a consistent pattern emerges: Alnylam dominates among neurologists while Pfizer dominates among cardiologists. This is the single most consistent finding in the entire dataset.

SOV Split by Specialty

Country AMVUTTRA SOV (Neuros) AMVUTTRA SOV (Cards) VYNDAQEL SOV (Neuros) VYNDAQEL SOV (Cards)
Spain (Q4 2024) 64% 52% 36% 48%
Japan (Q4 2024) 68% 34% 32% 66%
Germany (Q4 2024) 55% 43% 45% 57%
France (Q4 2023) ~62% (est.) ~42% (est.) ~38% ~58%
US (Q4 2024) Higher vs. cards Lower vs. neuros -- --

The cardiologist gap is Alnylam's primary commercial challenge internationally. In Japan, the SOV gap is 34 points between specialties (68% neuro vs. 34% cardio). Even in Spain, the strongest market, there is a 12-point gap. Given that ATTR-CM is the growth driver and cardiologists are the primary prescribers, closing this gap is the single highest-impact commercial lever.

Drivers of the Specialty Divide

  1. Heritage positioning: AMVUTTRA's RNAi lineage traces to ONPATTRO, which was a neurology product. Alnylam's field teams are historically neuro-oriented
  2. Message focus: Alnylam's most recalled messages center on PN efficacy, RNAi mechanism, and TTR knockdown -- messages that resonate more with neurologists than cardiologists
  3. Pfizer's cardiology infrastructure: Pfizer has a massive cardiology sales force from its broader CV portfolio. Their reach among cardiologists exceeds Alnylam in nearly every market
  4. ATTR-CM is newer: The ATTR-CM indication was approved only in March 2025. International markets are still adapting messaging and field deployment for the cardiology audience

Perception Paradox Among Cardiologists

An interesting sub-pattern: in Japan and Spain, when AMVUTTRA reps DO reach cardiologists, they drive stronger product perception improvement than they do among neurologists:

  • Japan: 91% cardiologists improved perception post-detail vs. 57% neurologists
  • Spain: 57% cardiologists improved perception vs. 71% neurologists (slightly reversed but still high for a cardio audience that was barely reached in W1)
  • Germany: 57% cardiologists vs. 87% neurologists

This suggests the problem is reach, not receptivity. Cardiologists who see an AMVUTTRA detail are being persuaded -- Alnylam just is not reaching enough of them.

Pattern 3: Message Effectiveness Trends

AMVUTTRA's Most Effective Messages (Cross-Country)

Across all markets, AMVUTTRA's highest-performing messages cluster around:

  1. Rapid TTR knockdown (88% mean reduction in <3 weeks): Highly motivating but often under-recalled. Average message effectiveness 62-73% across markets, but recall only 33-50%. This is a consistent delivery gap -- the most motivating message is not being delivered frequently enough.

  2. RNAi as first-line therapy for hATTR-PN: Strong recall and effectiveness in France and Spain (where PN is the primary indication tracked). Less relevant in cardiology-focused markets.

  3. Quarterly SC dosing convenience: Highly recalled across all markets (often the top-recalled message). Moderate effectiveness -- functional differentiator but does not drive emotional motivation.

  4. Favorable safety profile: Most recalled message in multiple markets but consistently rated as LEAST effective. This is a universal pattern: safety messages get delivered most but move the needle least.

The "Safety Trap"

In France (Q4 2023), AMVUTTRA's safety message A6 was the highest-recalled message at 63% but had the lowest message effectiveness. In Spain (Q4 2024), safety was recalled by 67% but the CM efficacy message was far more motivating. In Germany, tolerability was most recalled (58%) but rapid knockdown efficacy drove more prescribing intent.

Recommendation pattern visible across all countries: Reps are over-indexing on safety discussions and under-indexing on the rapid TTR knockdown narrative. The data consistently shows that efficacy-focused messages -- particularly the rapid knockdown story and CM-specific efficacy -- are most motivating to prescribe but least delivered.

VYNDAQEL's Message Strengths

VYNDAQEL's most effective messages across markets focus on:

  • All-cause mortality reduction (V1) -- strongest efficacy claim, consistently top-rated
  • Convenient once-daily oral dosing (V3) -- high recall, moderate effectiveness
  • Disease awareness/diagnosis messaging (V4, V5) -- strategic territory Pfizer occupies effectively

VYNDAQEL consistently achieves higher average message recall than AMVUTTRA (55% vs. 39-44% across most markets), reflecting Pfizer's higher visit frequency and established messaging cadence.

Pattern 4: Rep Performance -- Quality vs. Quantity

Alnylam Reps Win on Impact

Across nearly all markets and both waves, AMVUTTRA reps score higher than VYNDAQEL reps on:

  • Improvement in product perception (most pronounced gap)
  • Likelihood to increase prescription (significant in most markets)
  • Knowledge of ATTR/hATTR (rated highly across all markets)

Pfizer Reps Win on Activity

Across most markets, Pfizer reps lead on:

  • Overall call quality (particularly cardiologists)
  • Visit frequency (consistently higher)
  • Reach (especially among cardiologists)

Country-Specific Performance Patterns

France (Q2 2024, neuros only): AMVUTTRA reps drove strongest perception change (50%) and Rx intent (50%) vs. ONPATTRO (44%, 22%) and VYNDAQEL (38%, 25%). But Pfizer reps rated better for hATTR disease knowledge and addressing product questions -- a gap Alnylam needs to close.

Italy (Q2 2024): AMVUTTRA improved perception at 54% vs. VYNDAQEL at 54% (parity). But AMVUTTRA's NPS dropped from 43 to 23 between waves while VYNDAQEL dropped from 49 to 23 -- both declining, suggesting market fatigue or increased competitive noise.

Spain (Q4 2024): AMVUTTRA reps drove 71% improvement in perception (up from 58% in Q4 2023) -- the largest perception improvement gain in the dataset. VYNDAQEL perception improvement was 62% (up from 57%). Alnylam is pulling away in Spain.

Germany (Q4 2024): AMVUTTRA reps drove 67% perception improvement vs. 60% for VYNDAQEL. However, VYNDAQEL reps drove higher Rx intent (60% vs. 50% for AMVUTTRA) -- MDs may perceive AMVUTTRA favorably but translate this into prescribing action less for the oral alternative.

Japan (Q4 2024): AMVUTTRA reps drove 78% perception improvement vs. 57% for VYNDAQEL -- the largest gap in any market. Yet AMVUTTRA's lower reach means fewer physicians experience this impact.

Pattern 5: Evolving Competitive Landscape (US Q1 2025 Integration)

The US integrated analysis introduces complexity not seen in international markets:

Four-Way Competition

The US now tracks four manufacturers: Alnylam, AstraZeneca (Wainua), Pfizer, and BridgeBio (ATTRUBY). This makes the US competitive landscape significantly more complex than international markets where it is largely AMVUTTRA vs. VYNDAQEL.

ATTRUBY's Rapid Entry

The April 2026 check-in meetings reveal that ATTRUBY is already being actively tracked in PET with 90 interactions per quarter. The PCA data shows ATTRUBY combination charts (AMVUTTRA + ATTRUBY, WAINUA + ATTRUBY) are already appearing, indicating real-world combination use has begun.

Wainua Dynamics

Wainua fielding was paused in Q2 2026 after already exceeding quota (62 completes vs. 60 quota at 103%). This suggests either sufficient data saturation or a strategic decision to pause pending CARDIO-TTRansform readout.

US Rep Activity Trends (Q4 2024 vs. prior)

From the Q1 2025 integration deck, all manufacturers showed declining reach and frequency quarter-over-quarter:

  • Alnylam: -1 pt reach, -0.8 frequency
  • AstraZeneca: -4 pts reach, -0.9 frequency
  • Pfizer: -7 pts reach, -0.7 frequency
  • BridgeBio: +5 pts reach (new entrant, growing)

BridgeBio is the only manufacturer growing reach, consistent with a launch ramp. Pfizer's declining reach is notable and may signal strategic de-prioritization as tafamidis patent cliff approaches.

AMVUTTRA US Message Performance (Q4 2024)

AMVUTTRA message effectiveness in the US showed significant declines vs. prior quarter across nearly all messages (ME, Motivating, Believable, and Differentiation all declined 10-31 points). This is a concerning signal that could indicate:

  • Market saturation / diminishing impact of repeated messaging
  • Competitive noise from ATTRUBY and Wainua launches diluting attention
  • Need for message refresh to maintain differentiation

The access/affordability message O23 showed the sharpest decline (-29 pts on differentiation), suggesting competitors are eroding AMVUTTRA's access advantage perception.

Pattern 6: Target List and Segmentation Evolution

The April 2026 check-in meetings reveal a major methodological shift that will affect all future data:

New Target List Segmentation

Alnylam has implemented a new target list with HCP segmentation tiers: Ultra, High, Medium, Low, Exploratory. This replaces the simpler on-list/off-list binary. The new TL is significantly smaller:

  • Only 33% of unique HCPs from call lists are present in the new TL (vs. 66% in the old)
  • This creates a 56% reduction in available respondents for ATU recruitment and 72% reduction for PCA

Usage Segments

HCPs are now classified into three usage segments:

  • AMV users: Currently prescribing AMVUTTRA (32-57% of on-TL depending on indication)
  • Likely TTR Rxers: Not yet AMVUTTRA users but likely to prescribe (4-11%)
  • Stabilizer only: Currently using only stabilizers (39-57%)

Implications for Research

This segmentation enables more precise tracking of adoption curves. The "stabilizer only" segment is the conversion target -- physicians using tafamidis/ATTRUBY who have not yet tried AMVUTTRA. In the ATU Q2 2026 data, 14 of 39 evaluable cardiologists were "stabilizer only users," suggesting significant conversion opportunity.

Survey Length Pressure

The ATU survey is at 26 minutes (ATTR-CM baseline) with projected increase to 30-33 minutes after Q3 2026 changes (MaxDiff addition + Wainua loop). This is approaching the 35-minute upper limit. The team is evaluating tradeoffs between standardizing to MaxDiff methodology vs. accommodating all four product loops.

Pattern 7: Patient-Level Insights from PCA

The US PCA data (CM+Mixed+PN charts) provides the only patient-level view of treatment patterns:

Combination Therapy Is Real

PCA completes already show meaningful combination therapy patterns:

  • AMVUTTRA + VYNDAMAX: 24 charts (CM), 2 charts (PN)
  • AMVUTTRA + ATTRUBY: 16 charts (CM)
  • WAINUA + VYNDAMAX: 6 charts (Mixed), 1 chart (PN)
  • WAINUA + ATTRUBY: 3 charts (Mixed)

Silencer + stabilizer combinations are emerging as a real treatment paradigm, particularly for ATTR-CM patients. This validates Alnylam's strategic imperative 1.2 (establish AMVUTTRA as foundational therapy across treatment paradigm).

User Type Distribution for AMVUTTRA

Among physicians detailed on AMVUTTRA (both CM and PN):

  • 67-72% are current users
  • 22% are non-users
  • 6-7% are non-treaters

The 22% non-user segment receiving AMVUTTRA details represents the active conversion target. Tracking this segment's migration to user status over time would be a high-value longitudinal metric.

Cross-Cutting Recommendations

Based on patterns observed across all countries and time periods:

  1. Close the cardiologist reach gap: This is the single highest-impact lever across all international markets. AMVUTTRA's impact per detail is strong -- the problem is getting in front of more cardiologists.

  2. Shift message mix from safety to rapid knockdown: The data uniformly shows safety messages are over-delivered and under-performing. The rapid TTR knockdown narrative is motivating but recalled in only 33-50% of details. Every market would benefit from more frequent delivery of efficacy-focused messages.

  3. Watch US message fatigue: The sharp decline in US message effectiveness (Q4 2024) is a leading indicator that may presage similar trends internationally as competitive noise increases.

  4. Leverage the Spain playbook: Spain's successful SOV flip from 35% to 56% between waves provides a template for other trailing markets (Italy, Japan). Key elements: aggressive reach expansion, strong CM efficacy messaging, leveraging academic institutions.

  5. Prepare for Wainua ATTR-CM data: CARDIO-TTRansform results expected mid-2026 will either validate AMVUTTRA's silencer monopoly in ATTR-CM or create a second gene-silencing competitor. Either outcome changes the competitive messaging framework.

  6. Monitor combination therapy evolution: PCA data showing silencer+stabilizer combinations should be tracked as a leading indicator of treatment paradigm shifts. If combinations become standard of care, AMVUTTRA's positioning shifts from "replacement for stabilizers" to "foundational therapy added to stabilizers."

  7. Adapt to the new target list: The 56-72% reduction in available respondents from TL changes will affect sample composition and potentially trend comparability. Establish clear methodological bridges between old-TL and new-TL data to maintain longitudinal integrity.
